allow:
Bash(git commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
feat: Complete KSC Refiner v1.3 - Configuration-driven architecture redesign

Major Architectural Overhaul:

1. Configuration-Driven Design
   - Eliminate hardcoded row/column numbers
   - Central parser rules in parser_config.yaml
   - YAML-based flexible parser configuration
   - Easy addition of new companies via config

2. Modular Architecture
   ├── core/
   │   ├── schema.py           # Data models & validation
   │   ├── config_loader.py    # Configuration management
   │   ├── output_writer.py    # Excel output generation
   │   └── engine.py           # Main processing engine
   ├── parsers/
   │   ├── base_parser.py              # Base parser class
   │   ├── config_driven_parser.py     # Generic config-based parser
   │   ├── kctr_specialized_parser.py  # KCTR-specific parser
   │   └── parser_factory.py           # Parser factory pattern
   ├── config/
   │   ├── parser_config.yaml  # Parser rules (sheet names, mappings)
   │   └── rates.json          # Exchange rates
   └── utils/
       └── excel_helper.py     # Excel utilities

3. Key Improvements from v1.1/v1.2
   ✅ Remove code duplication (5 similar parsers → 1 generic + 1 specialized)
   ✅ Centralize year/sheet detection logic
   ✅ Enhance error handling with logging
   ✅ Simplify maintenance (config changes vs code changes)
   ✅ Better extensibility (new companies via YAML)

4. Parser Configuration Features
   - Sheet name priority lists for flexible matching
   - Row/column mappings per company
   - Support for both actual and plan data
   - Label-based matching for special cases (KCTR ★PL(Report))

5. Testing Results
   - Successfully extracted 596 records from 10 files
   - KCTR: 75 records (9 actual + 66 plan)
   - KSCCZ: 76 records (16 actual + 60 plan)
   - KSCE: 85 records (50 actual + 35 plan)
   - KSCP: 360 records (252 actual + 108 plan)
   - All 3 output sheets generated correctly

6. Documentation
   - Comprehensive README.md with architecture overview
   - Configuration examples
   - Troubleshooting guide
   - Version history

Design Philosophy:
- Configuration over code
- Modularity over monolithic design
- Logging over silent failures
- Flexibility over rigid rules

This version provides a solid foundation for long-term maintenance
and easy adaptation to changing Excel file formats.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")

Bash(git commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
feat: Add complete build system for KSC Refiner v1.3

Build System Components:

1. PyInstaller Configuration
   - ksc_engine.spec: Bundle Python engine into standalone .exe
   - Include config files (parser_config.yaml, rates.json)
   - Single-file executable with all dependencies

2. C# Launcher (ksc_launcher)
   - User-friendly GUI wrapper
   - Folder browser dialog for input selection
   - Year input dialog
   - Automatic output folder opening
   - Error handling and status messages
   - Target: .NET 8.0 Windows (PublishSingleFile)

3. C# Setup Launcher (ksc_setup)
   - Runs Inno Setup installer with admin privileges
   - Simplified installation experience
   - UAC prompt handling

4. Inno Setup Script (KscRefiner.iss)
   - Professional Windows installer
   - Desktop shortcut option
   - Program Files installation
   - Uninstaller creation
   - Korean language support

5. Automated Build Script (build.bat)
   - 4-stage build process:
     [1/4] PyInstaller: Python engine → ksc_engine.exe
     [2/4] dotnet publish: C# launcher → KscRefiner_v1.3.exe
     [3/4] File organization: Copy files to publish/
     [4/4] Inno Setup: Create installer package
   - Automatic dependency checking
   - Error handling at each stage
   - Explorer integration (auto-open publish folder)

Build Outputs:
- publish/ksc_engine.exe (Python engine)
- publish/KscRefiner_v1.3.exe (C# launcher)
- publish/config/ (configuration files)
- installer_output/KscRefiner_Setup_v1.3.0.exe (installer)

Documentation:
- Updated README.md with build instructions
- Windows user guide
- Developer build guide

Usage:
  Windows: Run build.bat
  Linux: Python engine only (no C# components)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")
Bash(git commit -m "fix: Convert build.bat to ASCII encoding to fix Windows execution")
